title: Daily Briefing — May 29
headline: Tech's running hot, but lagging sectors are flashing risk—flows, not FOMO, dictate next moves.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us: the market keeps marching higher, with SPY closing at $757.00 well above the 20 MA ($739.36)—no technical breakdowns here. VIX is normal and trending lower, and Fear & Greed sits at 60.3 (greed, but nowhere near bubble territory), so it's calm. Flows and momentum in XLK (Technology) are leading, but underlying breadth in lagging sectors is getting ugly.

Sector rotation is clear: XLK has RS=124.0, Mom=104.9—leading by miles, and most tech names (AAPL, MSFT, AMZN, META, ARM) are extended from their 20 MA. Meanwhile, XLE, XLF, XLP, XLV, and XLI are lagging hard, all with high risk ratings. The market remains narrow—breadth is soft, leadership concentrated, and this never lasts forever. If you lose financials (XLF $51.35 vs. 20 MA $51.50), economy sickness is on the table.

Contrarian radar: NVDA looks suspicious, sitting below its 20 MA ($215.62) with a bear bias—watch for institutions accumulating near $214. Energy (XLE, USO) and commodities are rolling over, with everything below 20 MAs (R006 triggered for exit, do not chase). Too many lagging sectors = fragility, but nothing's breaking yet. Patience: if bonds (HYG/LQD/TLT) start selling, that's your real risk signal. Retail FOMO is absent, but if press headlines start pumping 'soft landing,' archive it for later.
callout_body:
SPY: $757.00 (20 MA: $739.36) — bullish, momentum intact
NVDA: $214.38 (20 MA: $215.62) — below 20 MA, bear bias, flag for accumulation
XLK: $190.11 (20 MA: $176.04) — extended tech leadership
XLF: $51.35 (20 MA: $51.50) — flirting with mean, canary status
XLE: $56.50 (20 MA: $58.24) — exit, energy rolling over
VIX: 15.7 — calm regime, but don't let your guard down
action_items: Trail stops on tech leaders (AAPL, MSFT, AMZN, XLK) but avoid chasing—abundance mindset, wait for mean reversion.
Exit or reduce exposure in lagging/triggered sectors (Energy, Staples, Real Estate, NVDA)—R006 rule: daily close below 20 MA, exit longs.
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price—follow the flows, not the headlines.
